req.title: Break-glass access
req.statement: A clinician shall invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient; break-glass use shall be audited and trigger a post-event review.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-021 defines three testable outcomes: (1) a clinician with a valid token and an existing patient receives 200 + access + audit event on break-glass invocation (EP positive path); (2) a non-clinician role is denied access (NEG / DT — role column false); (3) a boundary case exercises the minimum-length justification string (exactly 1 character) to probe the BVA edge of the 'reason' field contract, ensuring the system either accepts or rejects deterministically. A fourth STC test covers the full state machine: normal-access → break-glass-invoked → audit-recorded → review-task-created. All tests are self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own patient resources.

tests.name: test_breakglass_clinician_valid_patient_grants_access_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Authenticated clinician JWT available via <clinician_token> fixture
Existing patient resource available via <existing_patient_id> fixture
Audit log is queryable via <get_latest_audit_event> helper
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician token, existing patientId, and non-empty reason
expected: Audit event with type BREAK_GLASS is emitted for the patient
Audit event records the clinician subject
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_breakglass_clinician_valid_patient_grants_access_and_emits_audit(
    base_url, clinician_token, existing_patient_id, get_latest_audit_event
):
    payload = {
        "patientId": existing_patient_id,
        "reason": "Cardiac arrest — immediate access required"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("accessGranted") is True, "accessGranted must be True"

    audit = get_latest_audit_event(patient_id=existing_patient_id, event_type="BREAK_GLASS")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit event not found"
    assert audit["eventType"] == "BREAK_GLASS"
    assert audit["subjectId"] == <clinician_subject_id>

tests.name: test_breakglass_non_clinician_role_is_denied
preconditions: Authenticated non-clinician (role: nurse_admin) JWT available via <nurse_admin_token> fixture
Existing patient resource available via <existing_patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /BreakGlass with nurse_admin token and existing patientId
expected: Response body contains an error indicating insufficient role
No audit BREAK_GLASS event emitted for this attempt

tests.name: test_breakglass_minimum_length_reason_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated clinician JWT available via <clinician_token> fixture
Existing patient resource available via <existing_patient_id> fixture
API contract specifies reason field minimum length >= 1 character
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician token, existing patientId, and reason of exactly 1 character
POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician token, existing patientId, and reason of empty string (below boundary)
expected: Request with 1-character reason returns HTTP 200 and accessGranted: true
Request with empty reason returns HTTP 400 with validation error
trace: R-HLT-021: clinician asserts emergency — reason field boundary at min length 1
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_breakglass_minimum_length_reason_boundary(
    base_url, clinician_token, existing_patient_id
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}

    # BVA lower bound: exactly 1 character — should be accepted
    resp_min = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/BreakGlass",
        json={"patientId": existing_patient_id, "reason": "E"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp_min.status_code == 200, (
        f"1-char reason should be accepted, got {resp_min.status_code}: {resp_min.text}"
    )
    assert resp_min.json().get("accessGranted") is True

    # BVA below lower bound: empty string — should be rejected
    resp_empty = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/BreakGlass",
        json={"patientId": existing_patient_id, "reason": ""},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp_empty.status_code == 400, (
        f"Empty reason should be rejected with 400, got {resp_empty.status_code}: {resp_empty.text}"
    )

tests.name: test_breakglass_state_transition_access_to_audit_to_review_task
preconditions: Authenticated clinician JWT available via <clinician_token> fixture
Existing patient resource available via <existing_patient_id> fixture
Patient record is in normal-access state (no active break-glass session)
Review task queue is queryable via <get_review_tasks> helper
actions: Assert patient has no active break-glass session
POST /BreakGlass to transition to break-glass-active state
Verify audit event is recorded (audit-recorded state)
Verify post-event review task is created (review-pending state)
expected: HTTP 200 and accessGranted: true after POST
State transitions: normal → break-glass-active → audit-recorded → review-pending
Audit event of type BREAK_GLASS exists for the patient
A review task referencing the patientId and the clinician exists in the task queue
trace: R-HLT-021: break-glass use shall be audited
R-HLT-021: error pathways — review task created
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_breakglass_state_transition_access_to_audit_to_review_task(
    base_url, clinician_token, existing_patient_id,
    get_latest_audit_event, get_review_tasks
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}

    # State 0: verify no active break-glass session exists
    pre_audit = get_latest_audit_event(patient_id=existing_patient_id, event_type="BREAK_GLASS")
    assert pre_audit is None, "Pre-condition failed: active BREAK_GLASS event already exists"

    # Transition: normal → break-glass-active
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/BreakGlass",
        json={"patientId": existing_patient_id, "reason": "Mass casualty triage"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json().get("accessGranted") is True

    # State: audit-recorded
    audit = get_latest_audit_event(patient_id=existing_patient_id, event_type="BREAK_GLASS")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit event must exist after break-glass invocation"

    # State: review-pending
    tasks = get_review_tasks(patient_id=existing_patient_id)
    assert any(t["type"] == "BREAK_GLASS_REVIEW" for t in tasks), "Review task not created"
